{"key_event":"Gran Colombia’s fragile post-independence consolidation","reasoning":"The new republican state inherited wartime institutions and formal central authority after independence, but its ability to tax, administer territory, and control regional caudillos remained weak and uneven. Civil society had some elite political mobilization, press, local councils, and factional organization, yet participation was narrow and constrained by hierarchy, war legacies, and limited institutional depth.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":3.5,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":3.5}
